title: Complexity, Profiling, Rendering Budgets, and Responsiveness · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed puzzle review reconstructs complexity, profiling, rendering budgets, and responsiveness from revision, configuration, seed, topology, frontier, predecessor map, state transition, draw record, accessibility report, profile, artifact, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for representative performance and responsiveness report after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Derive time and space from cells, passages, frontier, item count, algorithm, and representation before measuring.
Measure generation, solving, state conversion, drawing, callback delay, startup, save, and package behavior separately.
Define response, animation, cancellation, and redraw budgets and report percentiles or worst representative stalls.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Capacity Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review puzzle team handles case 1369 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate algorithm, render, and cancellation profiles under a representative responsiveness budget; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Set supported grid and action limits, reject excess early, compare representative devices, and name residual risks.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Capacity Defense.
steps.why: Retrieving Capacity Defense through the concrete representative performance and responsiveness report case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Capacity Defense, keep Capacity Defense connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Set supported grid and action limits, reject excess early, compare representative devices, and name residual risks.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Capacity Defense, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Capacity Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Capacity Defense, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A review puzzle team handles case 1369 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate algorithm, render, and cancellation profiles under a representative responsiveness budget; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Set supported grid and action limits, reject excess early, compare representative devices, and name residual risks.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Record what required effort · Review Capacity Defense, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: If a large maze eventually completes it is supported.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using supported and grid plus specific case evidence.
